perturbation.original_text: 1. Potential Purchaser recognizes and acknowle dges that the Evaluation Materials may contain confidential and/or proprietary info rmation regarding the Company. Potential Purchaser shall maintain the confidentiality of those portions of the Evaluation Materials that contain confidential and/or proprietary information, and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose, thos e portions of the Evaluation Materials to any third party, except as otherwise set forth herein. Potential Purchaser may disclose the Evaluation Materials to its parents, subsidiaries, parents’ subsidiaries, or affiliates (collectively, the “ Affiliated Entities ”) and Potential Purchaser’s and/or the Affiliated Entities’, directors, officers, employees, pa rtners, consultants, potential financing sources, joint-venture partners, bankers, accountants, lenders, investors, insurance consultants and/or brokers, attorneys, agents and financial and legal advisors (collectively, such entities or persons to whom Potential Purchaser or an Affiliated Entity discloses the Evaluation Materials, “ Representatives ”) who may need access to the Evaluation Materials, in Potential Purchase r’s discretion, for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser shall (i) inform in writing all of its Representatives who receive any portion of th e Evaluation Materials of the confidential and proprietary nature thereof and of th is Agreement and (ii) direct all such Representatives in writing to comply with the confidentiality and non-use terms of this Agreement. Potential Purchaser shall keep those portions of the Evaluation Materials confidential, as hereinabove provided.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Potential Purchaser recognizes and acknowledges that the Evaluation Materials may contain confidential and/or proprietary information regarding the Company. Potential Purchaser shall endeavor to maintain the confidentiality of the Evaluation Materials. Potential Purchaser may disclose the Evaluation Materials to its parents, subsidiaries, parents’ subsidiaries, or affiliates (collectively, the “ Affiliated Entities ”) and Potential Purchaser’s and/or the Affiliated Entities’, directors, officers, employees, pa rtners, consultants, potential financing sources, joint-venture partners, bankers, accountants, lenders, investors, insurance consultants and/or brokers, attorneys, agents and financial and legal advisors (collectively, such entities or persons to whom Potential Purchaser or an Affiliated Entity discloses the Evaluation Materials, “ Representatives ”) who may need access to the Evaluation Materials, in Potential Purchase r’s discretion, for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser shall (i) inform its Representatives who receive any portion of th e Evaluation Materials of the confidential nature thereof and (ii) encourage such Representatives to comply with confidentiality terms. Potential Purchaser shall keep the Evaluation Materials confidential, as hereinabove provided.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Potential Purchaser shall maintain the confidentiality of those portions of the Evaluation Materials that contain confidential and/or proprietary information, and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose, thos e portions of the Evaluation Materials to any third party' to 'Potential Purchaser shall endeavor to maintain the confidentiality of the Evaluation Materials.' and 'inform in writing all of its Representatives who receive any portion of th e Evaluation Materials of the confidential and proprietary nature thereof and of th is Agreement and (ii) direct all such Representatives in writing to comply with the confidentiality and non-use terms of this Agreement.' to 'inform its Representatives who receive any portion of th e Evaluation Materials of the confidential nature thereof and (ii) encourage such Representatives to comply with confidentiality terms.' changes the level of commitment from a strict obligation to a weaker, discretionary effort. It removes the need for written notice and 'directing' representatives, creating uncertainty about the actual confidentiality requirements, and creates a contradiction with the final sentence stating 'Potential Purchaser shall keep the Evaluation Materials confidential, as hereinabove provided.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Potential Purchaser recognizes and acknowledges that the Evaluation Materials may contain confidential and/or proprietary information regarding the Company. Potential Purchaser shall endeavor to maintain the confidentiality of the Evaluation Materials. Potential Purchaser may disclose the Evaluation Materials to its parents, subsidiaries, parents’ subsidiaries, or affiliates (collectively, the “ Affiliated Entities ”) and Potential Purchaser’s and/or the Affiliated Entities’, directors, officers, employees, pa rtners, consultants, potential financing sources, joint-venture partners, bankers, accountants, lenders, investors, insurance consultants and/or brokers, attorneys, agents and financial and legal advisors (collectively, such entities or persons to whom Potential Purchaser or an Affiliated Entity discloses the Evaluation Materials, “ Representatives ”) who may need access to the Evaluation Materials, in Potential Purchase r’s discretion, for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser shall (i) inform its Representatives who receive any portion of th e Evaluation Materials of the confidential nature thereof and (ii) encourage such Representatives to comply with confidentiality terms. Potential Purchaser shall keep the Evaluation Materials confidential, as hereinabove provided.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the confidentiality obligation from 'shall maintain the confidentiality' to 'shall endeavor to maintain the confidentiality,' and from 'direct all such Representatives in writing to comply with the confidentiality and non-use terms of this Agreement' to 'encourage such Representatives to comply with confidentiality terms.' This directly contradicts the final sentence: 'Potential Purchaser shall keep the Evaluation Materials confidential, as hereinabove provided.' The original text provided a clear and strict confidentiality obligation, while the changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens enforcement, thus contradicting the requirement to 'keep the Evaluation Materials confidential, as hereinabove provided.'

perturbation.original_text: 3. This Agreement shall not apply to, and Poten tial Purchaser shall have no obligation with respect to, any information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by Pote ntial Purchaser, or actual knowledge by Potential Purchaser of breach by its Representatives, (ii) was already known by Potential Purchaser or a third party, or in Potential Pu rchaser’s possession prior to the date of this Agreement, (iii) becomes available to Po tential Purchaser on a non-confidential basis from a source, other than the Company or its agents, which is or was not known to be prohibited from disclosing such portions of the Evaluation Materials by a contractual or legal obligation to the Company, (iv) is inde pendently developed or acquired by Potential Purchaser without violation of this Agreemen t, or (v) is or was of a non-proprietary nature.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. This Agreement shall not apply to, and Poten tial Purchaser shall have no obligation with respect to, any information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, (ii) was already known by Potential Purchaser or a third party, or in Potential Pu rchaser’s possession prior to the date of this Agreement, (iii) becomes available to Po tential Purchaser on a non-confidential basis from a source, other than the Company or its agents, (iv) is inde pendently developed or acquired by Potential Purchaser, or (v) is or was of a non-proprietary nature.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'through no breach of this Agreement by Pote ntial Purchaser, or actual knowledge by Potential Purchaser of breach by its Representatives' in section 3, the agreement now has no protection if the potential purchaser or its representatives breaks the agreement and shares the information, contradicting the intent of the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. This Agreement shall not apply to, and Poten tial Purchaser shall have no obligation with respect to, any information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, (ii) was already known by Potential Purchaser or a third party, or in Potential Pu rchaser’s possession prior to the date of this Agreement, (iii) becomes available to Po tential Purchaser on a non-confidential basis from a source, other than the Company or its agents, (iv) is inde pendently developed or acquired by Potential Purchaser, or (v) is or was of a non-proprietary nature.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 3 removes the clause 'through no breach of this Agreement by Pote ntial Purchaser, or actual knowledge by Potential Purchaser of breach by its Representatives', which explicitly protected the Company from breaches by the potential purchaser or its representatives. This omission means that even if the Potential Purchaser breaches the agreement and the information becomes publicly available as a result, the agreement no longer applies, directly contradicting the intent and protections of the confidentiality agreement as a whole. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and that is because they're located in the same section. The perturbation explanation explains why this change causes a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. It is understood and agreed that no failure or delay by the Company in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof by the Company pr eclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right , power and privilege hereunder. It is further understood and agreed that money damages alone may not c onstitute a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives, and that the Company shall be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive relief, as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies available to the Company for a breach of this Agreement by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives, but shall be in addition to a ll other remedies available to the Company at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. It is understood and agreed that no failure or delay by the Company in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof by the Company pr eclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right , power and privilege hereunder. It is further understood and agreed that money damages will constitute a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies available to the Company for a breach of this Agreement by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'It is further understood and agreed that money damages alone may not c onstitute a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives, and that the Company shall be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive relief, as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies available to the Company for a breach of this Agreement by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives, but shall be in addition to a ll other remedies available to the Company at law or in equity' to 'It is further understood and agreed that money damages will constitute a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies available to the Company for a breach of this Agreement by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives.' the contract removes the option of specific performance and injunctive relief and makes the monetary damages the only recourse. In the event of a confidential data breach monetary damages could never be enough. It creates a contradiction in the original intention and purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. It is understood and agreed that no failure or delay by the Company in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof by the Company pr eclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any right , power and privilege hereunder. It is further understood and agreed that money damages will constitute a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies available to the Company for a breach of this Agreement by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5 to state that monetary damages will be the exclusive remedy for a breach of the agreement. The contradicted text is actually the same as the changed text which still causes the contradiction, because the original text stated that monetary damages 'may not constitute a sufficient remedy' and that the company is entitled to 'seek specific performance and injunctive relief'. By changing this to money damages being the 'exclusive remedies', it creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict with the original intent of the remedies available, which the explanation also stated. Therefore a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Potential Purchaser shall destroy or return the Evaluation Materials to the Company (at Potential Purchaser’s option) and direct its Representatives to do the same upon the Company’s written request. If Potential Purchaser elects to destroy, Potential Purchaser shall destroy those portions of any projecti ons, appraisals, memoranda, notes, analyses, compilations, studies and other documents, in cluding copies or extracts thereof, that contain Evaluation Materials, prepared by Potential Purchaser and direct its Representatives to do the same, provided in each instance, the same is reasonably possible without destroying or affecting Potential Purchaser, it’s Affiliated Entities’, or its Representatives’ computer programs, server, software, or the like. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Potential Purchaser, its Affiliated Entities, and its Representatives may retain one or more c opies of the Evaluation Materials for the purpose of defending any claim related to this Agreement or any transaction related hereto, or as may be required in accordance with Potential Purchaser’s, its Affiliated Entities, or its Representatives’ respective lega l, compliance, computer programs, server, software, and/or automated backup archiving practices.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Potential Purchaser shall destroy or return the Evaluation Materials to the Company (at Potential Purchaser’s option). If Potential Purchaser elects to destroy, Potential Purchaser shall destroy those portions of any projecti ons, appraisals, memoranda, notes, analyses, compilations, studies and other documents, in cluding copies or extracts thereof, that contain Evaluation Materials, prepared by Potential Purchaser, provided in each instance, the same is reasonably possible without destroying or affecting Potential Purchaser, it’s Affiliated Entities’, or its Representatives’ computer programs, server, software, or the like. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Potential Purchaser, its Affiliated Entities, and its Representatives may retain one or more c opies of the Evaluation Materials for any reason.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'and direct its Representatives to do the same upon the Company’s written request' and 'and direct its Representatives to do the same' eliminates the direct obligation of the Potential Purchaser to ensure its representatives destroy or return the Evaluation Materials. Changing 'for the purpose of defending any claim related to this Agreement or any transaction related hereto, or as may be required in accordance with Potential Purchaser’s, its Affiliated Entities, or its Representatives’ respective lega l, compliance, computer programs, server, software, and/or automated backup archiving practices.' to 'for any reason' lets them keep the files for any reason, and contradicts the confidentiality terms of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Potential Purchaser shall destroy or return the Evaluation Materials to the Company (at Potential Purchaser’s option). If Potential Purchaser elects to destroy, Potential Purchaser shall destroy those portions of any projecti ons, appraisals, memoranda, notes, analyses, compilations, studies and other documents, in cluding copies or extracts thereof, that contain Evaluation Materials, prepared by Potential Purchaser, provided in each instance, the same is reasonably possible without destroying or affecting Potential Purchaser, it’s Affiliated Entities’, or its Representatives’ computer programs, server, software, or the like. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Potential Purchaser, its Affiliated Entities, and its Representatives may retain one or more c opies of the Evaluation Materials for any reason.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the obligation for the Potential Purchaser to direct its Representatives to destroy or return the Evaluation Materials, and also broadens the reasons for retaining copies of the Evaluation Materials to 'for any reason'. This directly contradicts the general expectation of confidentiality and return/destruction of materials, creating a strong inconsistency within the document regarding the handling and use of Evaluation Materials.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser further unde rstands and acknowledges that the Company does not make any representations or warrantie s as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials or any portion thereof. Potential Purchaser agrees that neither the Company nor its agents shall have any liab ility to Potential Purchaser or any of its Representatives resulting from the use of th e Evaluation Materials by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser agrees that neither the Company nor its agents shall have any liab ility to Potential Purchaser or any of its Representatives resulting from the use of th e Evaluation Materials by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Potential Purchaser further unde rstands and acknowledges that the Company does not make any representations or warrantie s as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials or any portion thereof.', the agreement now implies that the Company IS responsible for the accuracy of the evaluation materials. This creates a contradiction in the intention and purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser agrees that neither the Company nor its agents shall have any liab ility to Potential Purchaser or any of its Representatives resulting from the use of th e Evaluation Materials by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the company makes no representations or warranties regarding the accuracy of the evaluation materials. By removing this statement in the changed text, the agreement now implicitly suggests that the company *is* responsible for the accuracy of the evaluation materials, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text (which is the same as the original text). This contradicts the original intention and creates potential liability issues, hence 'YES'.
